class Solution:
    def minCost(self, nums: List[int], x: int) -> int:
        n = len(nums)
        min_cost = float('inf')
        
        # For each possible number of operations (up to n-1)
        for k in range(n):
            current_cost = 0
            # For each position i, find the minimum cost of the i-th type after up to k operations
            for i in range(n):
                # The i-th type can be found at position (i - k) % n after k operations
                # We need to find the minimum cost among all positions that can become the i-th type
                # after up to k operations
                min_val = float('inf')
                for j in range(k + 1):
                    pos = (i - j) % n
                    min_val = min(min_val, nums[pos])
                current_cost += min_val
            # Add the cost of performing k operations
            current_cost += k * x
            min_cost = min(min_cost, current_cost)
        
        return min_cost
